primary_meaning: something that you have succeeded in doing
common_collocations: great achievement, proud achievement, personal achievement, academic achievement
image_search_query: trophy or person celebrating success with raised arms
image_context: A gold trophy on a pedestal or a person with arms raised in victory celebrating an achievement
example_1: Graduating from university was her greatest achievement.
example_1_translation: Formar-se na universidade foi sua maior conquista.
example_2: The team celebrated their achievements at the ceremony.
example_2_translation: A equipe comemorou suas conquistas na cerimônia.
example_3: This award recognizes outstanding academic achievements.
example_3_translation: Este prêmio reconhece conquistas acadêmicas outstanding.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: a-CHIEVE-ment. Emphasize second: a-CHIEVE-ment
memory_hook: Achieve-ment - what you 'achieve' becomes your achievement

primary_meaning: a strong desire to achieve something
secondary_meaning: the thing you want to do or become
usage_notes: Use with 'ambition to', 'have the ambition', 'burning ambition'
common_collocations: great ambition, burning ambition, ambition to succeed, personal ambition
image_search_query: person climbing ladder or reaching for star representing ambition
image_context: A person climbing a tall ladder toward a star or reaching high for their dreams
example_1: Her ambition is to become a doctor.
example_1_translation: Sua ambição é se tornar médica.
example_2: He has the ambition to start his own business.
example_2_translation: Ele tem a ambição de abrir seu próprio negócio.
example_3: Political ambitions drove his decision.
example_3_translation: Ambições políticas guiaram sua decisão.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: am-BI-tion. Emphasize second: am-BI-tion
memory_hook: Am-bi-tion - 'ambi' (around) 'tion' (action) - wanting to go all around

primary_meaning: the quality of trying to do something despite difficulties
common_collocations: show determination, with determination, full of determination, resolve
image_search_query: person running马拉松 or climbing mountain showing determination
image_context: An athlete running a marathon or a climber ascending a steep mountain showing strong determination
example_1: Her determination helped her overcome many obstacles.
example_1_translation: A determinação dela a ajudou a superar muitos obstáculos.
example_2: He showed great determination to finish the race.
example_2_translation: Ele mostrou grande determinação para terminar a corrida.
example_3: With determination, anything is possible.
example_3_translation: Com determinação, tudo é possível.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: de-ter-mi-NA-tion. Emphasize third: de-ter-mi-NA-tion
memory_hook: De-ter-mi-na-tion - 'term' (end) 'in' - determining to reach the end

primary_meaning: the act of giving your time and energy to something
secondary_meaning: the commitment to a task or goal
common_collocations: show dedication, require dedication, dedication to work, great dedication
image_search_query: person working late at desk or focused on task showing dedication
image_context: A person working late at a desk with a lamp on or someone completely focused on their work
example_1: Her dedication to teaching inspires her students.
example_1_translation: A dedicação dela ao ensino inspira seus alunos.
example_2: Success requires dedication and hard work.
example_2_translation: O sucesso requer dedicação e trabalho duro.
example_3: He showed remarkable dedication to the project.
example_3_translation: Ele mostrou notável dedicação ao projeto.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ded-i-CA-tion. Emphasize third: ded-i-CA-tion

primary_meaning: the ability to recover quickly from difficulties
secondary_meaning: the capacity to adapt to challenges
common_collocations: show resilience, emotional resilience, build resilience, inner resilience
image_search_query: rubber ball bouncing back or tree bending in wind showing resilience
image_context: A rubber ball bouncing back after hitting the ground or a flexible tree bending but not breaking in a storm
example_1: The community showed great resilience after the disaster.
example_1_translation: A comunidade mostrou grande resiliência após o desastre.
example_2: Children often have remarkable resilience.
example_2_translation: As crianças geralmente têm resiliência notável.
example_3: Building resilience helps us cope with stress.
example_3_translation: Construir resiliência nos ajuda a lidar com o estresse.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: re-SIL-ience. Emphasize second: re-SIL-ience

primary_meaning: the ability to do something
secondary_meaning: the qualities that make someone able to do things
usage_notes: Use with 'capability of', 'have the capability', 'beyond capability'
common_collocations: have the capability, beyond capability, military capability, technical capability
image_search_query: superhero with powers or tool representing capability
image_context: A superhero displaying their powers or a set of tools and equipment representing various capabilities
example_1: The software has the capability to analyze large data sets.
example_1_translation: O software tem a capacidade de analisar grandes conjuntos de dados.
example_2: She has the capability to lead the team effectively.
example_2_translation: Ela tem a capacidade de liderar a equipe efetivamente.
example_3: We need to assess each candidate's capabilities.
example_3_translation: Precisamos avaliar as capacidades de cada candidato.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: cap-a-BIL-i-ty. Emphasize third: cap-a-BIL-i-ty

primary_meaning: special skills or knowledge in a particular area
common_collocations: expertise in, require expertise, technical expertise, medical expertise
image_search_query: expert professional with credentials or specialized tools
image_context: A professional with certificates on the wall or a specialist using advanced equipment
example_1: We need someone with expertise in marketing.
example_1_translation: Precisamos de alguém com conhecimento em marketing.
example_2: His expertise proved invaluable for the project.
example_2_translation: A expertise dele foi inestimável para o projeto.
example_3: She gained expertise through years of practice.
example_3_translation: Ela ganhou expertise através de anos de prática.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: ex-per-TISE. Emphasize third: ex-per-TISE

primary_meaning: the ability to do something well without wasting time
secondary_meaning: the ratio of output to input
common_collocations: improve efficiency, operational efficiency, energy efficiency, high efficiency
image_search_query: gear wheels working smoothly or fast assembly line representing efficiency
image_context: Well-oiled gears working in perfect harmony or a fast assembly line producing items efficiently
example_1: The new system improved our efficiency significantly.
example_1_translation: O novo sistema melhorou nossa eficiência significativamente.
example_2: We need to increase energy efficiency in our homes.
example_2_translation: Precisamos aumentar a eficiência energética em nossas casas.
example_3: The machine operates with remarkable efficiency.
example_3_translation: A máquina opera com eficiência notável.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ef-FI-cien-cy. Emphasize second: ef-FI-cien-cy

primary_meaning: the rate at which goods are produced
common_collocations: increase productivity, worker productivity, low productivity, productivity gains
image_search_query: factory production line or growing chart showing productivity
image_context: A busy factory production line or an upward trending graph showing productivity increases
example_1: The company aims to increase productivity by 20%.
example_1_translation: A empresa quer aumentar a produtividade em 20%.
example_2: Remote work has affected employee productivity.
example_2_translation: O trabalho remoto afetou a produtividade dos funcionários.
example_3: We need to address the issue of low productivity.
example_3_translation: Precisamos abordar a questão da baixa produtividade.
pronunciation_tips: Five syllables: prod-uc-TI-vi-ty. Emphasize third: prod-uc-TI-vi-ty
memory_hook: Pro-duc-ti-vi-ty - 'produce' activity - how much you produce

primary_meaning: a plan designed to achieve a goal
secondary_meaning: the art of planning and directing military movements
common_collocations: business strategy, marketing strategy, long-term strategy, develop strategy
image_search_query: chess board with strategic moves or roadmap with marked path
image_context: A chess board with pieces in strategic positions or a roadmap with a clearly marked path
example_1: We need a clear strategy to reach our goals.
example_1_translation: Precisamos de uma estratégia clara para alcançar nossos objetivos.
example_2: The marketing strategy was very effective.
example_2_translation: A estratégia de marketing foi muito eficaz.
example_3: His strategy is to focus on the core market.
example_3_translation: Sua estratégia é focar no mercado central.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: STRA-te-gy. Emphasize first: STRA-te-gy
common_mistakes: 'Strategy' not 'strategie' - has 'y' not 'ie' at the end

primary_meaning: the ability to take action without being told
usage_notes: Use with 'take initiative', 'on your own initiative', 'new initiative'
common_collocations: take initiative, new initiative, local initiative, personal initiative
image_search_query: person leading charge or person starting new project
image_context: A person taking the first step forward leading others, or someone breaking ground on a new project
example_1: She took the initiative to organize the event.
example_1_translation: Ela tomou a iniciativa de organizar o evento.
example_2: The government launched a new environmental initiative.
example_2_translation: O governo lançou uma nova iniciativa ambiental.
example_3: He should have more initiative in his work.
example_3_translation: Ele deveria ter mais iniciativa em seu trabalho.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: i-NI-tia-tive. Emphasize second: i-NI-tia-tive

primary_meaning: a piece of work that requires much effort over a period
usage_notes: Use with 'work on a project', 'start a project', 'project management'
common_collocations: work on a project, start a project, research project, group project
image_search_query: construction site or person working on multiple tasks representing project
image_context: A construction site with building plans or a person managing multiple tasks on a project board
example_1: The project is scheduled to finish next year.
example_1_translation: O projeto está programado para terminar no próximo ano.
example_2: She is leading the research project.
example_2_translation: Ela está liderando o projeto de pesquisa.
example_3: We need more funding for this project.
example_3_translation: Precisamos de mais financiamento para este projeto.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: PRO-ject. Emphasize first: PRO-ject
common_mistakes: 'Project' noun vs pro-JECT verb - pronunciation differs

primary_meaning: a course of action adopted by an organization
secondary_meaning: a principle or rule to guide decisions
common_collocations: company policy, government policy, privacy policy, foreign policy
image_search_query: official document or rulebook representing policy
image_context: An official binder full of policy documents or a gavel representing rule-making authority
example_1: The company has a strict privacy policy.
example_1_translation: A empresa tem uma política de privacidade rigorosa.
example_2: The government announced new environmental policies.
example_2_translation: O governo anunciou novas políticas ambientais.
example_3: It is company policy to arrive on time.
example_3_translation: É política da empresa chegar no horário.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: POL-i-cy. Emphasize first: POL-i-cy
common_mistakes: 'Policy' not 'policty' - has 'y' not 'ty' at the end

primary_meaning: a law or set of laws passed by parliament
common_collocations: pass legislation, new legislation, proposed legislation, environmental legislation
image_search_query: parliament building or law books representing legislation
image_context: The parliament building with flags or stacks of law books representing passed legislation
example_1: The government passed new legislation on data protection.
example_1_translation: O governo aprovou nova legislação sobre proteção de dados.
example_2: Environmental legislation is becoming stricter.
example_2_translation: A legislação ambiental está se tornando mais rígida.
example_3: The legislation requires all companies to report annually.
example_3_translation: A legislação exige que todas as empresas façam relatórios anuais.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: leg-is-LA-tion. Emphasize third: leg-is-LA-tion

primary_meaning: the ability to guide and motivate others
secondary_meaning: the position of being a leader
common_collocations: show leadership, leadership skills, strong leadership, under leadership
image_search_query: leader addressing group or captain guiding team
image_context: A leader standing at the front addressing a group or a captain guiding their team
example_1: She demonstrated excellent leadership during the crisis.
example_1_translation: Ela demonstrou excelente liderança durante a crise.
example_2: Leadership skills are important for managers.
example_2_translation: Habilidades de liderança são importantes para gerentes.
example_3: The company prospered under his leadership.
example_3_translation: A empresa prosperou sob sua liderança.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: LEADER-ship. Emphasize first: LEADER-ship

primary_meaning: the act of working with others on a project
common_collocations: collaboration with, international collaboration, close collaboration, scientific collaboration
image_search_query: people working together at table or joint puzzle representing collaboration
image_context: Multiple people gathered around a table working together or puzzle pieces fitting together
example_1: The collaboration between the two universities produced great research.
example_1_translation: A colaboração entre as duas universidades produziu ótima pesquisa.
example_2: Our team values collaboration over individual work.
example_2_translation: Nossa equipe valoriza a colaboração mais do que o trabalho individual.
example_3: International collaboration is essential for this project.
example_3_translation: A colaboração internacional é essencial para este projeto.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: col-lab-o-RA-tion. Emphasize third: col-lab-o-RA-tion

primary_meaning: a discussion to reach an agreement
common_collocations: enter negotiations, successful negotiation, wage negotiation, peace negotiation
image_search_query: two parties shaking hands or people at conference table
image_context: Two parties shaking hands over a table or diplomats at a conference table in discussion
example_1: The labor negotiation lasted several weeks.
example_1_translation: A negociação trabalhista durou várias semanas.
example_2: They entered into negotiations with the company.
example_2_translation: Eles entraram em negociações com a empresa.
example_3: Successful negotiation requires compromise from both sides.
example_3_translation: Negociação bem-sucedida requer Compromisso de ambos os lados.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ne-go-ti-A-tion. Emphasize third: ne-go-ti-A-tion

primary_meaning: the act of exchanging information
usage_notes: Use with 'communication with', 'good communication', 'means of communication'
common_collocations: good communication, means of communication, communication skills, open communication
image_search_query: speech bubbles or people talking on phones
image_context: Speech bubbles representing conversation or people talking on phones exchanging information
example_1: Good communication is essential for teamwork.
example_1_translation: Uma boa comunicação é essencial para o trabalho em equipe.
example_2: The internet has changed how we use communication.
example_2_translation: A internet mudou como usamos a comunicação.
example_3: She has excellent communication skills.
example_3_translation: Ela tem excelente habilidade de comunicação.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: com-mu-ni-CA-tion. Emphasize third: com-mu-ni-CA-tion

primary_meaning: a public statement about something
usage_notes: Use with 'make an announcement', 'public announcement', 'official announcement'
common_collocations: make an announcement, public announcement, official announcement, surprise announcement
image_search_query: microphone or megaphone representing announcement
image_context: A microphone on a stand or a person with a megaphone making an important announcement
example_1: The company made an official announcement about the merger.
example_1_translation: A empresa fez um comunicado oficial sobre a fusão.
example_2: We await further announcements from the government.
example_2_translation: Aguardamos mais comunicados do governo.
example_3: His announcement surprised everyone.
example_3_translation: O anúncio dele surpreendeu todos.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: an-NOUNCE-ment. Emphasize second: an-NOUNCE-ment

primary_meaning: an active disagreement between people
common_collocations: conflict with, resolve conflict, armed conflict, cultural conflict
image_search_query: people arguing or opposing forces representing conflict
image_context: Two people in heated argument or opposing forces clashing representing conflict
example_1: There was a conflict between the two departments.
example_1_translation: Houve um conflito entre os dois departamentos.
example_2: We need to resolve this conflict peacefully.
example_2_translation: Precisamos resolver este conflito pacificamente.
example_3: The armed conflict lasted for years.
example_3_translation: O conflito armado durou anos.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: CON-flict. Emphasize first: CON-flict
memory_hook: Con-flict - 'flict' (strike) against - clashing against each other
common_mistakes: 'Conflict' not 'confllict' - has only one 'f' and one 'l'

primary_meaning: the process of organizing and managing something
secondary_meaning: the group of people who manage an organization
common_collocations: public administration, university administration, under new administration, hospital administration
image_search_query: office building or people in suits managing paperwork
image_context: A large office building with government emblem or people in suits managing paperwork and files
example_1: She works in hospital administration.
example_1_translation: Ela trabalha na administração hospitalar.
example_2: The administration announced new rules.
example_2_translation: A administração anunciou novas regras.
example_3: Public administration requires specialized training.
example_3_translation: A administração pública requer treinamento especializado.
pronunciation_tips: Five syllables: ad-min-is-TRA-tion. Emphasize fourth: ad-min-is-TRA-tion

primary_meaning: a relationship where two or more people work together
secondary_meaning: a business owned by two or more people
usage_notes: Use with 'partnership with', 'form a partnership', 'business partnership'
common_collocations: partnership with, form a partnership, business partnership, strategic partnership
image_search_query: two hands shaking or business partners at desk
image_context: Two hands shaking in a business setting or two partners working together at a desk
example_1: The partnership between the companies was very successful.
example_1_translation: A parceria entre as empresas foi muito bem-sucedida.
example_2: They formed a strategic partnership to develop new products.
example_2_translation: Eles formaram uma parceria estratégica para desenvolver novos produtos.
example_3: The partnership has been running for ten years.
example_3_translation: A parceria existe há dez anos.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: PART-ner-ship. Emphasize first: PART-ner-ship
memory_hook: Part-ner-ship - 'ner' (associate) - the ship (condition) of being partners
